spec_match.rationale: Builder TALKS about Chrome 137 no-fragment root resolution but the live <use> calls all target inline #icon-name symbols in a same-document <svg id="inline-sprite"> — i.e. same-document fragment lookups that have always worked. The generated code in the textarea shows the new form, but nothing in the live preview exercises it.

interactive.rationale: Real palette/canvas/size-slider/colour-picker; placing icons regenerates HTML output.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index has two paragraphs, one describing a side-by-side lane that does not exist in the page. Trim the second blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: Canvas / placement / clear flow is solid, but no handling for external file URLs, contrast warnings, or failure modes.

references.rationale: Page has NO `references` or `see also` block — no chromestatus link, no spec link. Mandatory per CLAUDE.md §3.

design_system.rationale: Inline `<input type="color" value="#1a1a1a">` seeds a raw hex and the chosen colour is dropped into output without a contrast check; the page also redefines the global button rule redundantly.

openQuestions.title: Add a references section with the chromestatus link
openQuestions.detail: Append a see-also section linking https://chromestatus.com/feature/5128141573718016 (mandatory for the slug self-heal script) and the SVG2 UseElement spec.

openQuestions.title: Make the live preview exercise the v137 path
openQuestions.detail: Today palette icons render via `<use href="#icon-star">` against an inline `<svg id="inline-sprite">` — same-document fragment lookup, not v137. Move each icon into its own data: URL (or static .svg file) and use bare-URL <use href="...">.

openQuestions.title: Trim the dangling second blurb in the feature index
openQuestions.detail: The index has a second paragraph for this concept describing side-by-side lanes that do not exist. Either delete or build them.

summary: Polished builder UI that demonstrates the wrong thing — every live <use> targets an inline same-document symbol. Wire the canvas to an external (or data:) URL and add the missing references section.
